body.title: [sprint] Verify filesystem ticket store is complete vs Postgres (per-ID, gate cutover)
body.description:
**Problem:** cc_queue.py's `_project_to_memory` (line 378) is fail-open — when projection fails the error is swallowed, so the filesystem store may be SILENTLY MISSING exactly the tickets where projection broke. Count parity (2077 FS vs ~2073 PG) does NOT prove per-ID completeness. Before any code trusts the filesystem as authoritative, every PG ticket must have a verified filesystem counterpart.

**Proposed shape:** A one-shot audit script: read all ticket IDs from clan.memories (parent_id=TICKETS_ROOT) + devlab.tickets; read all ticket IDs from devlab/runtime/memory/tickets/ (+ closed/); diff per-ID. Report PG-only IDs (the silent-drop risk) and FS-only IDs (newer, expected). Backfill any PG-only ticket into the filesystem store via the canonical emit path. Exit non-zero if any PG ticket lacks an FS counterpart after backfill.

**Affected files:** NEW devlab/claudecode/ticket_fs_completeness_audit.py (read-only + backfill); reads cc_queue.py storage constants (TICKETS_ROOT_ID).
**Design rules:** NO SQLITE. Postgres read is allowed here (this is the migration bridge). Log the per-ID diff counts (interface crossing).
**Scope boundary:** IN — per-ID diff PG vs FS, backfill PG-only tickets, fail if incomplete. OUT — changing cc_queue.py read/write path (that's T-cc-queue-fs-first); deleting PG (that's T-ticket-pg-drop).
**Completion criteria:** Script run reports 0 PG-only ticket IDs after backfill; output committed as evidence. This ticket GATES T-cc-queue-fs-first's flip-to-FS-authoritative and T-ticket-pg-drop.
**Test plan:** Run the audit; assert exit 0 and 0 PG-only IDs. Spot-check 3 backfilled tickets (if any) load correctly from the filesystem.
body.result: Gate PASSED. Audit (devlab/claudecode/ticket_fs_completeness_audit.py): PG=2073, FS=2083, PG-only=0 (no silent drops), FS-only=10 (newer FS-native). Filesystem store is complete vs Postgres; cutover may proceed.
